tool_name: Warehouse 3D Cube Utilisation, Pallet Position Count & Storage Capacity Analysis

inputs.name: Net Storage Area Ratio
inputs.hint: Gross area minus: docks, offices, charging bays, columns. Typical: 50–65% for conventional rack

inputs.name: Clear Stacking Height (under sprinkler/beam)
inputs.hint: Usable height after deducting sprinkler clearance (min 0.5m) and lighting

inputs.name: Pallet Length
inputs.hint: EUR pallet: 1.20m | ISO pallet: 1.20m | US GMA: 1.22m

inputs.name: Pallet Width
inputs.hint: EUR pallet: 0.80m | ISO pallet: 1.00m | US GMA: 1.02m

inputs.name: Loaded Pallet Height (pallet + load)
inputs.hint: EUR pallet 0.15m + load. Standard consumer goods: 1.0–1.5m | Industrial: 1.5–2.0m

inputs.name: Beam / Safety Clearance per Level
inputs.hint: Vertical clearance between top of load and next beam: min 100mm per SEMA / FEM 10.2.02

inputs.name: Operating Aisle Width
inputs.hint: Counterbalance FLT: 3.5–4.0m | Reach truck: 2.6–3.0m | VNA (VFLT): 1.6–1.8m | Manual: 1.5m

inputs.name: Racking / Storage System Type
options: Double-deep rack — access factor 0.5 (50% directly accessible)

inputs.name: Storage Selectivity Factor
inputs.hint: From system type above: Selective=1.0 | Double-deep=0.5 | Drive-in=0.25

inputs.name: Target Storage Occupancy Rate
inputs.hint: Operating above 85% causes pick congestion. WMS best practice: 80–85% max

formulas: Rack_Bay_Footprint_m2 = (pallet_length_m + aisle_width_m / 2) * pallet_width_m   // [m²] Footprint per pallet position (incl. aisle share)
Floor_Positions = FLOOR(Net_Storage_Area_m2 / Rack_Bay_Footprint_m2)   // [positions] Ground-level pallet locations
Height_per_Level_m = loaded_pallet_height_m + rack_beam_clearance_m   // [m] Vertical pitch per rack level
Max_Rack_Levels = FLOOR(clear_height_m / Height_per_Level_m)   // [levels] Maximum stacking levels within clearance
Total_Pallet_Positions = Floor_Positions * Max_Rack_Levels   // [positions] Total rack positions
Accessible_Positions = FLOOR(Total_Pallet_Positions * selectivity_factor)   // [positions] Directly accessible positions
Required_Height_m = Max_Rack_Levels * Height_per_Level_m   // [m] Height required by rack structure
Theoretical_Volume_m3 = gross_floor_area_m2 * clear_height_m   // [m³] Total building envelope volume
Storage_Volume_m3 = Total_Pallet_Positions * (pallet_length_m * pallet_width_m * loaded_pallet_height_m)   // [m³] Actual inventory volume
Operating_Positions = FLOOR(Total_Pallet_Positions * (occupancy_target_pct / 100))   // [positions] Usable positions at target occupancy
Positions_per_m2 = Total_Pallet_Positions / gross_floor_area_m2   // [positions/m²] Storage density KPI
Aisle_Area_m2 = gross_floor_area_m2 * (net_storage_ratio_pct / 100) * (aisle_width_m / (pallet_length_m + aisle_width_m))   // [m²] Estimated aisle area

standards: SEMA Code of Practice for Static Racking

height_exceeds_clear.error_msg: PHYSICAL IMPOSSIBILITY: Required rack height exceeds available clear height. Reduce rack levels, decrease loaded pallet height, or increase building clear height.

net_storage_ratio_max.error_msg: Net storage ratio > 75% is physically unrealistic. Minimum space is needed for: dock doors, charging bays, staging areas, columns, fire exits. Maximum achievable: 70% for automated warehouses.

aisle_minimum.error_msg: Aisle width < 1.5m is below minimum safe passage for any powered truck. Fire safety regulations also require minimum 1.2m emergency egress width.

smart_warnings.message: WIDE AISLE SYSTEM (≥3.5m): Aisle width suitable for counterbalance forklift only. Approximately 35–40% of floor area is dedicated to aisles. Switching to reach truck (aisle 2.6–2.8m) or VNA (1.6–1.8m) could increase pallet positions by 20–40% in the same building.

POOR CUBE UTILISATION (<25%): Building volume is severely underused. Root causes: insufficient rack height, too many aisles, low stacking, or low occupancy. World-class warehouses achieve 40–60% cube utilisation. Evaluate: (1) Taller racking, (2) Narrower aisles, (3) Double-deep or mobile racking.

OCCUPANCY TARGET > 90%: Operating above 90% causes severe congestion, pick path blockages, and forces multiple product relocations per pick. WMS systems degrade significantly above 85%. Required buffer for operational flexibility: minimum 15% empty positions.

Only 1–2 rack levels achievable: Building clear height is limiting vertical density. If building modification is not feasible, consider mezzanine floor installation or multi-tier picking to improve m³ utilisation.

Storage density < 0.8 positions/m² gross: Below industry benchmark for conventional racking (target: 1.0–1.5 positions/m²). Analyse: wide aisle ratios, low stacking height, and low net storage ratio as primary contributors.

Low accessibility + small absolute accessible count: With drive-in or pushback systems, FIFO rotation becomes operationally difficult. For perishable goods, pharmaceuticals, or date-coded products, minimum selectivity factor 0.8 is required to ensure FEFO compliance.
